prompt:
Our station's overnight automation runs a compliance sweep over each block's setlist before it airs — it is supposed to pull anything explicit outside safe harbor and anything longer than the slot's per-track cap, keeping the rest in running order. Two incidents this week, possibly the same problem: (1) whenever the sweep actually has something to pull, it dies with java.util.ConcurrentModificationException and the block airs unswept; blocks with nothing to pull sail through fine. (2) Tuesday night it did NOT crash, but an explicit track still aired at 2 a.m. — the two flagged tracks were back-to-back at the end of the block, the pull log claimed exactly one pull, and the block's closer stayed in the list. Board op filed both tickets; I can reproduce both on demand with the same setlists.

`java TestMain.java` has those exact fixtures (plain JDK source launcher, no build step). Make the sweep pull what it is supposed to pull, keep running order for everything that survives, report the pulled titles in original order, and get every test green — without touching TestMain.java.
